question: Read: 'Machines which gather, store and analyse data and can upload it to the Internet' — this is the definition of ________.
'Smart devices' are described as ________.
choices: devices embedded in the human body
electronic devices that connect to the internet and can be controlled remotely
devices that can only be used at home

question: Read: 'The company expects their invention to be launched next year.' This uses the ________.
Read: 'Fresh Brew was created by a British coffee company.' Why is the agent mentioned in this passive sentence?
choices: It is obvious who created it
It is unknown who created it
It is new and important information

question: In the smart doorbell advert, complete: 'Our Smart Doorbell ________ at our factory in Portugal.' (manufacture)
In the smart doorbell advert, complete: 'The on-board camera ________ to see who's at your door.' (can/use)
Read: 'In Eastern Europe, the number of devices went up quite dramatically.' Rewrite using Pattern 2: 'There was ________ in the number of devices in Eastern Europe.'
Which phrase correctly describes a downward trend?
choices: There has been a steady increase
The number of users declined sharply

question: In the article 'Early Adopter or Laggard?', 'backed up' means ________.
choices: stored on a device so it won't be lost

question: According to the Active Vocabulary section, which pair of words have 'different connotations' even though they both mean 'not modern'?
Read: 'This laptop is nearly ten years old.' Laila describes it as ________.
Read: 'Very few of our new laptops have CD drives as CDs are virtually ________.'
Read from the VR article: 'But for others, they end up having a meltdown — in other words, they get upset or angry.' The phrasal verb 'end up' means ________.
choices: to finally find yourself in a situation or doing something
to break off from an activity

question: Read from the VR article: 'This can make it difficult to fit in with or to keep friends.' 'Fit in with' means ________.
choices: to insert yourself into a crowd
to be accepted as part of a group
to adjust your behaviour for others

question: According to the VR article, the National Autistic Society's VR experience was designed to help people understand what ________ feels like.
In the phrasal verbs exercise, 'I have never really felt part of the group at school' should be replaced with ________.
The phrasal verb 'bump into' means ________.
Read: 'People started taking selfies to manage and curate what they wanted to tell the world about their physical attributes.' The word 'attributes' refers to ________.
choices: physical or personal qualities or characteristics

question: Read: 'Can someone's flaws make them more likeable? Yes, because imperfections can make a person seem more ________ and relatable.'
Read: 'On a superficial level, their holiday photos look relaxed and natural, but in reality they've been very carefully planned — and paid for.' What does this passage reveal?
choices: That holiday photos are always natural and genuine
That social media appearances can be carefully constructed and misleading
That planning photos is always a good idea
That photographers always produce natural-looking photos

question: Read: 'The more photos like dangerous selfies appear on social media, the more some people are encouraged to do something similarly dangerous themselves.' This means that ________.
choices: dangerous selfies should be reported immediately
liking or admiring dangerous selfies can encourage others to take similar risks
dangerous selfies are always removed by social media platforms
only young people take dangerous selfies

question: Complete: 'My piano teacher was very ________ about my progress, which made me feel great.'
Read: 'The designer is known to have been heavily influenced by early computers.' Which impersonal passive pattern is used?
Read: 'It is thought that the city's schools will be completely paperless within 50 years.' Which pattern is used?
Read: 'Of the nearly two billion websites believed to exist today, it is estimated that less than ________ million are actually active.'
Read: 'Social media users are reported to upload ________ million photographs every day.'
Read: 'Rather sadly, it is said that 70% of them are never ________ by anyone.'
In the for-and-against essay writing task, students are told to use ________ where possible and include phrases of contrast.

Read from 'Our Personal Data': 'Another positive aspect of data tracking is that people can be automatically logged onto a site, rather than having to enter a password.' What does 'automatically logged on' mean?
choices: Having to type your password every time
Being signed in to a website without manually entering your details
Creating a new account each visit
Using face recognition to enter a site

question: In the for-and-against essay ACTIVE WRITING section, it says a good essay should 'introduce different perspectives and critically ________ them'.
Combine: 'Many cookies are harmless' + 'it should be possible to use sites without accepting them' using 'nevertheless'. Which is correct?
choices: Many cookies are harmless although it should be possible to use sites without accepting them
Many cookies are harmless. Nevertheless, it should be possible to use sites without accepting them
Many cookies are harmless. In spite of it should be possible to use sites
Many cookies are harmless, whereas it should be possible to use sites without them

question: According to the Life Skills section on social media, you tend not to read about people when they are ________ or doing nothing.
According to the Life Skills text, to protect your privacy on social media, you should only allow ________ to see what you post.
choices: your friends or fellow students you can trust

question: In the revision passive exercise, complete: 'I ________ up by the neighbour's faulty burglar alarm again last night.' (wake)
In the revision passive exercise, 'The factory ________ just been automated, so some people have lost their jobs.' Which form is correct?
In the revision USE OF ENGLISH exercise, 'Some people find it difficult to ________ a job.'
Read: 'If someone else posted something negative about you, you can try contacting the site owner.' This advice is given for when ________.
choices: you want to delete your own posts
another person has posted something embarrassing about you
you want to create a new online identity
your privacy settings have been changed

question: In the Life Skills box, Tip 2 'Take steps to hide anything negative' is matched with ________.
choices: Remember that posting privately doesn't protect you
Set up an alert to let you know when someone mentions you
Add a lot of new posts to move something you don't want people to see further down search results
Don't share anything that belongs to someone else without permission

question: Read: 'Essentially, you are paying more to have your device first and of course, it will eventually become obsolete.' The word 'essentially' means ________.
choices: at a basic or fundamental level
in an unusual or unexpected way
in a detailed and thorough way

question: Read: 'New devices often come with compatibility issues, so that you also need to buy new adaptors or cables.' What is a 'compatibility issue'?
choices: A problem with the device's battery life
A problem that occurs when a new device cannot work with existing equipment
A fault that prevents a device from switching on
A software update that must be installed
